name: Cañón de la Utrera — circular por el río Manilva
region_intro: Hiking around Río Manilva offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths to rugged inland areas. The region is characterized by the dramatic Manilva River Canyon with its turquoise pools and karst formations, alongside rolling hills and Mediterranean forests. Hikers can explore ancient Roman baths, unique limestone landscapes like Sierra de Utrera, and natural riverside pools. This varied landscape provides a range of natural features for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Río Manilva The most popular hiking route is Cañón de la Utrera — circular por el río Manilva, a 6.1 miles (9.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route navigates through the impressive Manilva River Canyon, featuring rugged rock faces and lush vegetation. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of the Gorge – Manilva River Canyon loop from Cancón, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.6 km) path. This trail offers expansive views of the gorge and the surrounding Manilva River Canyon. Local hikers also love the Manilva River Canyon – View of the Gorge loop from Manilva, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail leading through the Manilva River Canyon, often completed in about 1 hour 31 minutes. Hiking around Río Manilva is defined by river canyons, limestone formations, and Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside strolls to more challenging inland excursions. The routes in Río Manilva are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 5000 hikers have used komoot to explore Río Manilva's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Río Manilva?
A: Río Manilva offers a diverse network of over a dozen hiking trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find options ranging from gentle riverside strolls to more challenging inland excursions through canyons and hills.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, Río Manilva has several easy trails perfect for families or those new to hiking. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14571226" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Manilva River Canyon – View of the Gorge loop from Manilva&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.4-mile path. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31541308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hedionda Roman Baths – Roman Bridge of Casares loop from Manilva&lt;/a&gt;, which is just 1.5 miles long.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the dramatic Manilva River Canyon with its turquoise pools and rugged rock faces, impressive karst formations in areas like Sierra de Utrera, and lush Mediterranean forests. Keep an eye out for natural riverside pools such as the Charco de la Paloma and the picturesque waterfalls at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6144687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hell's Pool&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. A prominent historical site is the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4078869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hedionda Roman Baths&lt;/a&gt;, dating back over two millennia. Several trails incorporate or start near these sulfurous waters, offering a unique blend of history and nature. You can experience them on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31551926" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hedionda Roman Baths – Canuto de la Utrera loop from Manilva&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Río Manilva?
A: Yes, many trails in Río Manilva are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1319183836/canon-de-la-utrera-circular-por-el-rio-manilva" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de la Utrera — circular por el río Manilva&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 6.1-mile loop through the impressive canyon.

Q: What is the terrain like for hiking in Río Manilva?
A: The terrain is quite varied, ranging from coastal paths with sea views to rugged inland areas. You'll encounter river canyons, rolling hills, and unique limestone formations. Some sections, particularly in the canyons, may involve navigating rocky passages, while others offer smoother paths through Mediterranean forests and agricultural lands.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many of the natural trails in Río Manilva. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife habitats, and to carry water for them. Please check local signage for any specific restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Río Manilva?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty of the river canyon, the unique geological formations, and the opportunity to combine outdoor activity with historical exploration at the Roman baths. The diverse landscapes and varied trail options are also highly appreciated.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls or natural pools for swimming?
A: Yes, the Río Manilva is known for its beautiful natural pools and waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6144687" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hell's Pool&lt;/a&gt; (Charco del Infierno) is an idyllic spot with picturesque waterfalls nestled within a karstic ravine, though reaching it may require some agility. The Charco de la Paloma is another notable natural pool easily identified by an ancient irrigation channel.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Río Manilva?
A: The best time to hike in Río Manilva is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) when temperatures are milder and the vegetation is lush. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable. Winter offers pleasant conditions, though some trails might be wetter after rainfall.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217131" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Start of the Hike – Sierra de Líbar Refuge loop from Casares&lt;/a&gt; offer a more demanding experience. This difficult 6.2-mile route involves significant elevation gain, providing a strenuous workout and rewarding views.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of the gorge?
A: Absolutely. The region is famous for its dramatic gorges. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38217604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Gorge – Manilva River Canyon loop from Cancón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 5.4-mile path specifically designed to offer expansive views of the gorge and the surrounding Manilva River Canyon.
tips: An incredible hiking area and rock climbing paradise, dubbed the 'puddle of hell', highlighting the waters of the Manilva River curving geometric shapes into the rocky formation of the canyon.

An area of exceptional natural beauty, the Manilva River Canyon is a must-hike if you visit this area. The waters of the river have been curving incredible rock formations until the mouth of the river that connects the Mediterranean between San Luis de Sabinillas and Marina de Casares.
The Romans' love of bathing and using water was celebrated in the Manilva River. This incredible natural landscape has many pools for you to enjoy.
